elements.text: Hey

Welcome to Scaletopia once again, and thanks for joining our Slack channel! We'll be using this space to communicate about all aspects of the campaigns and keep you updated along the way.

Once we receive the onboarding form, we can schedule a quick kick-off call to go through it together and align on what niches to go after and specific messaging angles will work best. After that, we'll start creating the copies and lead lists, with the goal of getting a campaign live within 7 days!

In the meantime, here is our entire delivery team that will be working on Kynship's outbound campaigns and the designated role each will be owning:
Myself - Campaign strategy & messaging & account management
- Lead list & Clay enrichments
- Campaign setup & execution
- Inbox management & replies
Really looking forward to working with you!

Hi All,

Great to be connected - I'm waiting on some team members to finish the case study portion of the onboarding forms - it may be early next week before we get that data - but i think it will be worth it rather than me stabbing and guessing at those figures (I'm newer to the company).

My goal is to be your easiest client - so if there is anything you see missing, additional questions please continue to ask until you have absolute clarity. The success of this partnership is KEY to my next 6-12 months here at Kynship - so feel free to give feedback on the resources etc or ask if you need anything else.

Thanks

elements.text: Hey

Good afternoon! Just wanted to give you a quick update on where we're at.

Call mining progress:
• 28 strategy calls mined
• 24 audit calls mined

Niches where I'm seeing the most patterns:
• Food & Bev / Consumables (energy drinks, supplements, sports nutrition)
• Beauty & Personal Care (skincare, scalp care, clean beauty)
• Health & Wellness (period care, supplements, barefoot footwear)
• UK brands looking to break into the US

Outbound infra:
• Email - lookalike domains are warming up, should be fully live by April 17th
• SMS - aiming to launch Tuesday next week, maybe even Monday

Couple check-ins:
1. Is there any update on the case studies? That's really the missing piece for me to figure out which niche to prioritise, the messaging angles to lock and draft the actual copies.
2. Also, what does your availability look like for Thu or Fri to hop on a 30 min kick-off call? Just want to make sure we're aligned before things go live.

Thanks!

text: Hi all - sheet is fully updated (or at least as much as i could get from the performance team).

elements.text: Hey
, thanks for the call! Great kicking things off with you today.
What we covered:
Niche selection:

Leading with cleaning/household goods (Purdy and Fig as the anchor case study) and either supplements or beauty/personal care as the second niche. Food and beverage moves down the priority list based on your feedback around lower AOV and profitability challenges in that space.
Messaging angles:

Leaning into what actually differentiates Kynship: profit-first ad buying, cost controls instead of "pay to test," and the creative volume math (2-3% hit rate across 2,000+ ads, so more creative = faster winners). We'll translate those into punchy outbound copy rather than trying to force them into a single label.
Purdy and Fig positioning:
For US prospects, the "$50M in the UK alone, now launching stateside" framing hits hard. UK email campaigns can reference them more directly once those domains are warmed up starting the 17th.
Launch plan:

SMS campaign goes live Tuesday/Wednesday, email launches from the 17th once domains are warmed. All copy comes to you for review before anything goes out. Weekly dashboard reports every Monday, bi-weekly syncs same time every two weeks.
Inactive positive leads:
Any positive replies that go inactive will be compiled into a list and handed over so your team can drop them into a nurture sequence.
Action items:
Us (Scaletopia):
Draft copy for the first niche sent to you by Monday
Checking with our ops team on the HubSpot integration for auto-creating deals (no rush, month two or three)
Look into pulling a lead list from your co-founders' Twitter followers via Clay
You (Kynship):
If you land on a name or shorthand for the profit-first ad buying approach, send it over and we'll work it into copy
Excited to get those first campaigns out the door next week, let's go book some meetings.

text:
Hey <@U09MU4BQWQP>, thanks for the call! Great kicking things off with you today.

*What we covered:*
• Niche selection: Leading with cleaning/household goods (Purdy and Fig as the anchor case study) and either supplements or beauty/personal care as the second niche. Food and beverage moves down the priority list based on your feedback around lower AOV and profitability challenges in that space.
• Messaging angles: Leaning into what actually differentiates Kynship: profit-first ad buying, cost controls instead of "pay to test," and the creative volume math (2-3% hit rate across 2,000+ ads, so more creative = faster winners). We'll translate those into punchy outbound copy rather than trying to force them into a single label.
• Purdy and Fig positioning: For US prospects, the "$50M in the UK alone, now launching stateside" framing hits hard. UK email campaigns can reference them more directly once those domains are warmed up starting the 17th.
• Launch plan: SMS campaign goes live Tuesday/Wednesday, email launches from the 17th once domains are warmed. All copy comes to you for review before anything goes out. Weekly dashboard reports every Monday, bi-weekly syncs same time every two weeks.
• Inactive positive leads: Any positive replies that go inactive will be compiled into a list and handed over so your team can drop them into a nurture sequence.
*Action items:* 

Us (Scaletopia):
• Draft copy for the first niche sent to you by Monday
• Checking with our ops team on the HubSpot integration for auto-creating deals (no rush, month two or three)
• Look into pulling a lead list from your co-founders' Twitter followers via Clay 
You (Kynship):
• If you land on a name or shorthand for the profit-first ad buying approach, send it over and we'll work it into copy
Excited to get those first campaigns out the door next week, let's go book some meetings.

elements.text: Hey

- Good afternoon!

Just wanted to leave a quick note on where we're at.
What we did last week:
Niche selection - we Selected Beauty & Skincare + Cleaning/household niches and we're working on scraping lead lists for each
Lead lists - our technical & delivery team have begun the lead scraping process and will have the list ready for this week's campaign launches
Copy angles - I've identified 3 unique differentiators to use
P&L Forecasting:
We back into your real CPA from your full P&L.
Cost Guardrails:
We run 100% on cost controls tied to your margins.
More Creative Testing:
We flood 100-300+ new creatives monthly, no testing-scaling split.
Plan for this week:
SMS - we will be launching our SMS campaign by starting with Beauty & Skincare by Wed (15th) and then Cleaning/household goods by Friday (17th)
Email - we will launch both Beauty & Skincare + Cleaning/household goods campaigns on Friday (17th)
Share the copy draft with you by tomorrow
Speak to you soon.

text:
Hey <@U09MU4BQWQP> - Good afternoon!

Just wanted to leave a quick note on where we're at.

*What we did last week:*
1. Niche selection - we Selected Beauty & Skincare + Cleaning/household niches and we're working on scraping lead lists for each
2. Lead lists - our technical & delivery team have begun the lead scraping process and will have the list ready for this week's campaign launches
3. Copy angles - I've identified 3 unique differentiators to use
    a. *P&L Forecasting:* We back into your real CPA from your full P&L.
    b. *Cost Guardrails:* We run 100% on cost controls tied to your margins.
    c. *More Creative Testing:* We flood 100-300+ new creatives monthly, no testing-scaling split.
*Plan for this week:*
1. SMS - we will be launching our SMS campaign by starting with Beauty & Skincare by Wed (15th) and then Cleaning/household goods by Friday (17th)
2. Email - we will launch both Beauty & Skincare + Cleaning/household goods campaigns on Friday (17th)
3. Share the copy draft with you by tomorrow 
Speak to you soon.

elements.text: Hey

, hope you had a great weekend!

Here are last week's updates:
Last Week's Metrics
Outreach: 1,408 total (475 email | 933 SMS)
Positive Replies: 3 (2 email | 1 SMS) Meetings Booked: 0
Positive replies
Jenna Levine - CEO of skincare brand, Linne
Ryan Zinn - Dr Bronners (
drbronner.com
Christina Flach - CEO of
Prettygirlmakeup.com
Performance Update

Not the start I was hoping for as I was looking forward to more meetings on the calendar. Based on the 2 campaigns we've run, it seems that the differentiation angle is not resonating with the audience (it needs to be much simpler).

However, we've got PRs from good brands and email has performed much better - Email landed 1 positive reply per 238 sends this week, which is a good sign. The angle and targeting combination on the email side is clearly resonating.

SMS is the weak side here. 933 sends produced only 1 positive reply (Jenna Levine), which is roughly 4x less efficient than email on this account. The locked seeding to paid pipeline angle isn't translating on SMS the way it does on email.
We're treating this week as a pure SMS rebuild.
Action Steps & What's Next
Test 2 to 3 new SMS hooks & drastically simplifying the SMS copy to land more PRs and meetings booked.
Following up on the PRs to convert into meetings booked

text:
Hey <@U09MU4BQWQP>, hope you had a great weekend!

Here are last week's updates:

*Last Week's Metrics*
• Outreach: 1,408 total (475 email | 933 SMS) 
• Positive Replies: 3 (2 email | 1 SMS) Meetings Booked: 0
Positive replies
• Jenna Levine - CEO of skincare brand, Linne
• Ryan Zinn - Dr Bronners (<http://drbronner.com|drbronner.com>)
• Christina Flach - CEO of <http://Prettygirlmakeup.com|Prettygirlmakeup.com> 
*Performance Update*
• Not the start I was hoping for as I was looking forward to more meetings on the calendar. Based on the 2 campaigns we've run, it seems that the differentiation angle is not resonating with the audience (it needs to be much simpler).
• However, we've got PRs from good brands and email has performed much better - Email landed 1 positive reply per 238 sends this week, which is a good sign. The angle and targeting combination on the email side is clearly resonating.
• SMS is the weak side here. 933 sends produced only 1 positive reply (Jenna Levine), which is roughly 4x less efficient than email on this account. The locked seeding to paid pipeline angle isn't translating on SMS the way it does on email.
We're treating this week as a pure SMS rebuild.

*Action Steps & What's Next*
• Test 2 to 3 new SMS hooks & drastically simplifying the SMS copy to land more PRs and meetings booked.
• Following up on the PRs to convert into meetings booked

elements.text:
Yes, I'll share the copies before we set the campaign live

For the case studies, although the Purdy and Figg revenue numbers are useful but as the SMS campaign will only be for US/Canada, it would just help to have another case study with equally relevant figures (revenue or no. of leads) for an A/B test. Wildbird figures on the website have percentages and from our data using that just doesn't work.

Also, I'd want to test 2 niches (ideally) in parallel so we can find the winning angle a lot sooner.

As for the DNC list, yes. I've received your email and will forward to my team.

I'll send you an invite for Thursday based on your open slots soon. Thanks

elements.text: Hi

, just a quick note to explain - this is an automated notification I sent with one click. I use these when I need your input (e.g. help crafting a reply) or to flag an action needed from your side.

In this case, the lead replied asking what our smallest retainer looks like since they’re not near the size mentioned in the outreach. Could you send me the right pricing/positioning to use?
